admissions.course_rigor: All of our programs reflect the University’s commitment to experiential education and interdisciplinary coursework and are taught by renowned faculty composed of leaders and visionaries within their respective fields. Drexel has a wide variety of majors and minors that will allow you to explore your individual interests and make an impact in the world around you.

projects_research: Research & Innovation Research & Innovation Institutes & Initiatives Innovation & Entrepreneurship Industry Engagement Global Partnerships

evidence.source_excerpt: A Drexel education will encourage you to explore your interests and allow you to develop your own path. With over 100 majors and as many minors, you're sure to find a program that speaks to you. Through our cross-disciplinary model of learning, you'll also have the opportunity to explore a diverse range of minors outside of your chosen major, allowing you to expand the breadth and depth of your knowledge and get the most from your education. No matter what you choose to study, your classes will be taught by accomplished faculty, many of whom are working professionals teaching in their area of expertise.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 5 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Drexel University' (legacyId=1521) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 5 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.

warnings: Major titles still need manual follow-up; no reliable official title list was extracted in this pass.
Phase 5 auto-enrichment completed using official-school web crawl heuristics; manual spot-checking is still recommended for edge cases.
GPA policy was normalized from absence of an explicit minimum-GPA statement on the cited official pages.
Testing policy needs manual confirmation; the crawler did not find a clean official testing-policy sentence.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Drexel University' (legacyId=1521) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Wikipedia list/category candidates ranked by trailing-365-day Wikipedia pageviews.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.
